openmeter/notification/adapter.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL persistence layer for the notification domain, implementing notification.Repository against four Ent entities (NotificationChannel, NotificationRule, NotificationEvent, NotificationEventDeliveryStatus). Every method body is wrapped in entutils.TransactingRepo / TransactingRepoWithNoValue so the ctx-carried Ent transaction is honored.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo wrapper on every method
patterns.description: Every adapter method defines an inner fn closure and passes it to entutils.TransactingRepo (value returns) or TransactingRepoWithNoValue (error-only). Never call a.db outside a fn wrapper.
patterns.example: fn := func(ctx context.Context, a *adapter) (*notification.Channel, error) { ... }; return entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, fn)

patterns.name: Soft-delete via SetDisabled + SetDeletedAt
patterns.description: Channels and rules are soft-deleted: SetDisabled(true) + SetDeletedAt(clock.Now()). All list queries filter with Or(DeletedAtIsNil(), DeletedAtGT(clock.Now())).

patterns.name: EagerLoadActiveChannels / EagerLoadRulesWithActiveChannels
patterns.description: Shared query-modifier functions (rule.go, event.go) filter channels to non-disabled and non-deleted at a given time. Use these whenever loading rule/event channel edges.

patterns.name: EntityMapping functions in entitymapping.go
patterns.description: All DB-to-domain conversions go through pure functions: ChannelFromDBEntity, RuleFromDBEntity, EventFromDBEntity, EventDeliveryStatusFromDBEntity. Never map inline in CRUD methods.

patterns.name: entdb.IsNotFound -> notification.NotFoundError
patterns.description: After any query, check entdb.IsNotFound(err) and return notification.NotFoundError{NamespacedID:...}; never surface raw Ent errors.
patterns.example: if entdb.IsNotFound(err) { return nil, notification.NotFoundError{NamespacedID: models.NamespacedID{Namespace: params.Namespace, ID: params.ID}} }

patterns.name: Bulk delivery-status creation inside CreateEvent transaction
patterns.description: CreateEvent saves the event, re-queries the rule with EagerLoadActiveChannels, then CreateBulk inserts one NotificationEventDeliveryStatus per active channel — all in one TransactingRepo fn.

patterns.name: Payload serialized as JSON string column
patterns.description: NotificationEvent.Payload is a string. CreateEvent json.Marshal(params.Payload) before SetPayload; EventFromDBEntity calls eventPayloadFromJSON which validates EventPayloadMeta.Version for invoice events.

key_file_guides.role: Config (Client, Logger), New() constructor, adapter struct with Tx/WithTx/Self for entutils transaction plumbing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: WithTx creates a txClient via entdb.NewTxClientFromRawConfig — must stay in sync with entutils.TxDriver contract. Self() is required by the TransactingRepo generic constraint.

key_file_guides.role: Pure DB-to-domain mappers; eventPayloadFromJSON does version check for invoice events.
key_file_guides.watch_for: A new EventType must be handled in the eventPayloadFromJSON switch or it silently deserializes without version check. entitymapping_test.go guards JSON shape.

key_file_guides.role: ListEvents and CreateEvent; CreateEvent saves event, fetches rule+channels, bulk-creates delivery statuses in one TransactingRepo.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New event types must handle nil channel edges in CreateEvent. EagerLoadRulesWithActiveChannels is exported for reuse.

key_file_guides.role: NotificationRule CRUD; UpdateRule ClearChannels().AddChannelIDs() replaces channel set atomically. Defines EagerLoadActiveChannels.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreateRule and UpdateRule re-query channels after Save — Ent Save does not return edge data.

key_file_guides.role: NotificationChannel CRUD with orderBy switch and pagination.
key_file_guides.watch_for: IncludeDisabled=false adds Disabled(false) filter; omitting leaks disabled channels.

key_file_guides.role: ListEventsDeliveryStatus, GetEventDeliveryStatus, UpdateEventDeliveryStatus — all in TransactingRepo.
key_file_guides.watch_for: UpdateEventDeliveryStatus uses SetOrClearNextAttemptAt for nullable NextAttemptAt — SetNextAttemptAt would panic on nil.

anti_patterns: Calling a.db directly outside a TransactingRepo/TransactingRepoWithNoValue fn closure — bypasses ctx transaction
Returning raw entdb errors instead of notification.NotFoundError for not-found cases
Mapping DB rows inline in CRUD methods instead of using entitymapping.go helpers
Loading rule channels without EagerLoadActiveChannels — includes disabled or deleted channels
Editing eventPayloadFromJSON to skip version validation for new invoice event types

decisions.decision: Soft-delete instead of hard-delete for channels and rules
decisions.rationale: In-flight events reference rule and channel IDs; hard deletion breaks FK integrity and orphans delivery status records.

decisions.decision: Bulk delivery-status insert inside CreateEvent transaction
decisions.rationale: Atomicity: an event with no delivery statuses would never dispatch; same-TX creation prevents a window where the event exists with no pending deliveries.

decisions.decision: Version check only for invoice event types in eventPayloadFromJSON
decisions.rationale: Invoice payload schema changed; version 0 (missing field) must be rejected to avoid deserializing stale JSONB into the new InvoicePayload shape.

code_examples.scenario: Add an adapter method returning a domain value inside a transaction

openmeter/notification/consumer.purpose: Watermill-based Kafka consumer for the notification service, routing system-topic events (entitlement snapshots, billing invoice created/updated) to typed handlers that call notification.Service.CreateEvent. Each new event type requires a new grouphandler registration in consumer.go.

patterns.name: grouphandler.NewNoPublishingHandler with typed GroupEventHandlers
patterns.description: consumer.go wires all event types in a single NewNoPublishingHandler call, each wrapped in grouphandler.NewGroupEventHandler[T]. Unknown event types are silently dropped.

patterns.name: EntitlementSnapshotHandler dispatches both threshold and reset
patterns.description: entitlementsnapshot.go routes to handleAsSnapshotEvent (balance threshold) and handleAsEntitlementResetEvent (reset) based on predicates; both call notification.Service.CreateEvent.

patterns.name: Dual V1+V2 dedup hash query for balance threshold events
patterns.description: Balance threshold events dedup by querying ListEvents with DeduplicationHashes containing both V1 (sha256) and V2 (xxh3 with ThresholdKind prefix). New events created only when no matching hash exists in the current usage period.

patterns.name: EventPayloadVersionCurrent required for invoice payloads
patterns.description: Invoice EventPayload must include EventPayloadMeta{Type: eventType, Version: notification.EventPayloadVersionCurrent}. Missing Version causes adapter deserialization to reject the payload.
patterns.example: payload := notification.EventPayload{EventPayloadMeta: notification.EventPayloadMeta{Type: eventType, Version: notification.EventPayloadVersionCurrent}, Invoice: &notification.InvoicePayload{Invoice: apiInvoice}}

patterns.name: Annotation-based event metadata
patterns.description: Created events carry annotations (AnnotationEventSubjectKey, AnnotationEventFeatureKey, AnnotationEventCustomerID, AnnotationBalanceEventDedupeHash) set from the snapshot/invoice before CreateEvent.

patterns.name: Invoice handler skips gathering invoices
patterns.description: InvoiceEventHandler.Handle returns nil early when event.Invoice.Status == billing.StandardInvoiceStatusGathering — gathering invoices produce no notification events.

key_file_guides.role: Wires the Watermill router, instantiates handler structs, registers all event handlers. Run(ctx) starts the router; Close() stops it.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new event type requires a new grouphandler.NewGroupEventHandler here; forgetting it means the event is silently ignored.

key_file_guides.role: Dispatcher calling handleAsSnapshotEvent (threshold) and handleAsEntitlementResetEvent (reset); both can fire on the same SnapshotEvent.
key_file_guides.watch_for: isBalanceThresholdEvent gates processing — non-metered entitlements and deletes are skipped; event.Value must not be nil.

key_file_guides.role: Core balance threshold logic: rule filtering, dedup hash V1/V2, last-event comparison, event creation. Contains BalanceEventDedupHash, getActiveThresholdsWithHighestPriority.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Percentage thresholds require TotalAvailableGrantAmount > 0 (else ErrNoBalanceAvailable). ThresholdKind is part of V2 hash — changing the mapping is a breaking dedup change.

key_file_guides.role: Handles reset events: lists rules of type EventTypeEntitlementReset, dedups by existing event in current period, creates EntitlementReset payloads.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Dedup checks for any event in the current usage period — simpler than threshold dedup but still period-scoped.

key_file_guides.role: Handles StandardInvoiceCreatedEvent/UpdatedEvent; maps via billinghttp.MapEventInvoiceToAPI, creates one event per active rule.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Gathering invoices skipped explicitly; rules with Disabled=true skipped with a warning log.

anti_patterns: Adding a new event type handler without registering it in consumer.go's NewNoPublishingHandler — event is silently dropped
Setting Version to anything other than notification.EventPayloadVersionCurrent in invoice EventPayloadMeta
Changing BalanceEventDedupHash field composition without migrating V1/V2 hash constants — breaks dedup
Calling notification.Service methods without propagating msg.Context()
Triggering notification events for gathering invoices (Status == StandardInvoiceStatusGathering)

decisions.decision: Dual V1+V2 dedup hash query for balance threshold events
decisions.rationale: V2 adds ThresholdKind to prevent usage and balance threshold events from aliasing; V1 is queried alongside for backward compatibility with pre-V2 events.

decisions.decision: activeThresholds struct separates Usage and Balance thresholds
decisions.rationale: Usage (value/percent) and balance thresholds are independent; the highest active of each kind is selected separately so both can fire concurrently.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new system-event handler to the consumer

openmeter/notification/httpdriver.purpose: HTTP handler layer for the notification v1 API (channels, rules, events, delivery status), implementing httptransport.Handler[Req,Resp] and HandlerWithArgs. Delegates all business logic to notification.Service; only decodes requests, encodes responses, and maps errors.

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandler / NewHandlerWithArgs for every endpoint
patterns.description: Each CRUD operation returns a typed Handler. Decoder extracts namespace via h.resolveNamespace(ctx) and maps API params to service Input; operation calls h.service.<Method>; encoder uses commonhttp.JSONResponseEncoderWithStatus.

patterns.name: Type alias pattern for Request/Response/Params per endpoint
patterns.description: Each handler file declares type aliases: <Verb><Noun>Request = notification.<Verb><Noun>Input, Response = api.<ResponseType>, Handler = httptransport.Handler[...].
patterns.example: type (ListChannelsRequest = notification.ListChannelsInput; ListChannelsResponse = api.NotificationChannelPaginatedResponse; ListChannelsHandler httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[...])

patterns.name: Custom errorEncoder() in errors.go
patterns.description: All handlers pass httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(errorEncoder()); the encoder maps notification domain errors (NotFoundError) to correct HTTP status codes.

patterns.name: FromChannel / FromRule / FromEvent mapping functions in mapping.go
patterns.description: All domain-to-API conversions live in mapping.go. Never inline domain-to-API conversion in handler files.
patterns.example: item, err = FromChannel(channel); item, err = FromRule(rule)

patterns.name: namespace resolution via h.resolveNamespace(ctx)
patterns.description: Every decoder calls h.resolveNamespace(ctx) to get the tenant namespace — never read namespace from request body or path parameters.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return ListChannelsRequest{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve namespace: %w", err) }

key_file_guides.role: Defines Handler interface (all endpoint methods), handler struct (service, options, resolveNamespace), New() constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New endpoints must be added to the Handler interface before implementing the method.

key_file_guides.role: errorEncoder mapping notification domain errors to HTTP status codes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: notification.NotFoundError must map to 404; missing an error type falls through to 500.

key_file_guides.role: Domain-to-API converters (FromChannel, FromRule, FromEvent) and API-to-domain helpers (AsChannelWebhookCreateRequest, AsRuleBalanceThresholdCreateRequest).
key_file_guides.watch_for: FromRule dispatches on rule.Type — add a case for every new EventType. mapping_test.go covers payload shape; new payload types need test cases.

key_file_guides.role: Rule CRUD handlers plus TestRule (generates a synthetic event via internal.TestEventGenerator and calls CreateEvent).
key_file_guides.watch_for: TestRule accesses h.testEventGenerator — ensure it is injected in New() when wiring.

anti_patterns: Putting business logic in decoder/encoder functions instead of delegating to notification.Service
Inlining domain-to-API mapping in handler files instead of using mapping.go functions
Reading namespace from request body instead of h.resolveNamespace(ctx)
Omitting httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(errorEncoder()) from handler options
Returning non-API types from handler operations — encoder expects api.* response types

decisions.decision: Type aliases for Request/Response/Handler types per endpoint
decisions.rationale: Makes handler signatures self-documenting and lets the router reference concrete handler types without importing service input types directly.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new list endpoint following the established handler pattern

openmeter/notification/internal.purpose: Internal test-helper package that generates synthetic notification event payloads (balance threshold, entitlement reset, invoice) for notification rule tests and test event dispatch — never imported from production code paths.

patterns.name: TestEventGenerator struct with injected billing.Service
patterns.description: The sole exported type wraps billing.Service to generate realistic invoice payloads via billing.SimulateInvoice; entitlement payloads are built from hardcoded fixtures.

patterns.name: EventGeneratorInput.Validate() before switching on EventType
patterns.description: Generate() validates the input before branching, surfacing empty namespace or event type errors early.
patterns.example: if err := in.Validate(); err != nil { return notification.EventPayload{}, err }; switch in.EventType { ... }

patterns.name: Invoice payloads via billing.SimulateInvoice + billinghttp.MapEventInvoiceToAPI
patterns.description: newTestInvoicePayload calls the real billing simulation path and maps through the HTTP driver converter, keeping fixtures aligned with production serialization.
patterns.example: invoice, _ := t.billingService.SimulateInvoice(ctx, billing.SimulateInvoiceInput{...}); eventInvoice, _ := billing.NewEventStandardInvoice(invoice); apiInvoice, _ := billinghttp.MapEventInvoiceToAPI(eventInvoice)

key_file_guides.role: Entire package. Defines TestEventGenerator, EventGeneratorInput, and three private payload builders (balance threshold, entitlement reset, invoice).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Entitlement/balance payloads use hardcoded ULID strings and timestamps — update fixture literals if payload schemas change. New event types in Generate() must set EventPayloadMeta.Type correctly or callers misroute.

anti_patterns: Importing this package from production code — it pulls in billing HTTP driver types that have no place in live dispatch
Extending Generate() for a new event type without setting EventPayloadMeta.Type inside the returned payload
Replacing billing.SimulateInvoice with hand-crafted invoice structs — the simulation path keeps the payload structurally valid

decisions.decision: Use billing.SimulateInvoice for invoice test payloads instead of static fixtures
decisions.rationale: Invoice API shape is complex and changes often; the real simulation + HTTP mapping keeps the test payload always valid and aligned with production serialization.

openmeter/notification/service.purpose: Concrete implementation of notification.Service, orchestrating channel and rule lifecycle against both notification.Repository (Ent/PostgreSQL) and webhook.Handler (Svix). All multi-step mutations use transaction.Run / RunWithNoValue; Svix webhook operations run inside the same transaction scope as adapter writes.

patterns.name: transaction.Run wraps all multi-step mutations
patterns.description: CreateChannel, DeleteChannel, UpdateChannel, CreateRule, DeleteRule, UpdateRule wrap their bodies in transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, fn) so adapter and webhook calls share a transaction.
patterns.example: return transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx context.Context) (*notification.Channel, error) { channel, _ := s.adapter.CreateChannel(ctx, params); _, _ = s.webhook.CreateWebhook(ctx, ...); return channel, nil })

patterns.name: params.Validate() before every mutation
patterns.description: Every Service method calls params.Validate() (or ValidateWith) before any adapter/webhook call, wrapping the error without touching the DB.
patterns.example: if err := params.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid params: %w", err) }

patterns.name: Webhook adapter kept in sync with channel/rule lifecycle
patterns.description: CreateChannel calls webhook.CreateWebhook then adapter.UpdateChannel (store SigningSecret). DeleteChannel calls webhook.DeleteWebhook before adapter.DeleteChannel. CreateRule/DeleteRule call UpdateWebhookChannels per channel.
patterns.example: wb, _ := s.webhook.CreateWebhook(ctx, webhook.CreateWebhookInput{...}); updateIn.Config.WebHook.SigningSecret = wb.Secret; channel, _ = s.adapter.UpdateChannel(ctx, updateIn)

patterns.name: ValidateRuleChannels generic constraint
patterns.description: ValidateRuleChannels[T] used in CreateRule/UpdateRule calls s.adapter.ListChannels to verify referenced channels exist in the namespace before saving.

patterns.name: ChannelIDsDifference for minimal webhook updates on UpdateRule
patterns.description: UpdateRule computes notification.NewChannelIDsDifference(params.Channels, oldChannelIDs) and only calls UpdateWebhookChannels for Additions or Removals.
patterns.example: channelIDsDiff := notification.NewChannelIDsDifference(params.Channels, oldChannelIDs); if !channelIDsDiff.HasChanged() { return s.adapter.UpdateRule(ctx, params) }

key_file_guides.role: Defines Service struct (adapter, webhook, feature, logger), Config, New(), ListFeature delegating to feature.FeatureConnector.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New dependencies must be added to Config and validated in New(). Compile-time assertion var _ notification.Service = (*Service)(nil).

key_file_guides.role: ChannelService methods. CreateChannel stores the channel first, then creates the Svix webhook and updates SigningSecret back into the DB.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DeleteChannel blocks deletion if the channel is assigned to any rule (returns NewGenericValidationError with rule IDs). UpdateChannel forbids type change.

key_file_guides.role: RuleService methods. UpdateRule computes channel diffs and calls UpdateWebhookChannels only for changed assignments.
key_file_guides.watch_for: webhook.IsMaxChannelsPerWebhookExceededError must be checked after UpdateWebhookChannels; if hit, return NewGenericValidationError (not a 500).

anti_patterns: Calling adapter methods for multi-step mutations outside transaction.Run — partial writes if a webhook call fails mid-transaction
Skipping params.Validate() before adapter calls — invalid inputs reach the DB
Calling webhook.DeleteWebhook after adapter.DeleteChannel — leaves a dangling Svix webhook if DB delete succeeds but webhook delete fails
Deleting a channel without checking for assigned rules first — leaves rules referencing a deleted channel
Calling UpdateWebhookChannels for all channels on UpdateRule instead of only the diff — causes spurious Svix API calls

decisions.decision: Webhook operations performed inside the same transaction.Run as adapter writes
decisions.rationale: Channel/rule lifecycle must stay consistent with Svix; same-transaction scope means a webhook API failure rolls back the DB change rather than leaving them out of sync.

decisions.decision: ChannelIDsDifference for minimal Svix calls on UpdateRule
decisions.rationale: Svix enforces MaxChannelsPerWebhook; calling UpdateWebhookChannels for every channel on every update would hit the limit and cause spurious validation errors.

code_examples.scenario: Create a rule with webhook channel sync inside a transaction
